seo.title: Invoicemonk vs Dext: Receipt Management Comparison | 2026
seo.description: Compare Invoicemonk and Dext for receipt scanning and expense management. Features, pricing, and which tool is best for freelancers and small businesses.

hero.title: Invoicemonk vs Dext
hero.subtitle: Dext is a receipt processing powerhouse — but do you need a standalone tool, or does an all-in-one platform serve you better?

overview: Dext (formerly Receipt Bank) built its reputation on one thing: turning paper receipts into structured financial data with industry-leading OCR accuracy. For accountants managing dozens of clients and processing thousands of receipts monthly, Dext is a proven, reliable tool. Its integrations with Xero, QuickBooks, and Sage make it a natural fit in the accounting software ecosystem.

But here's the thing: Dext is only a receipt and expense capture tool. It doesn't send invoices. It doesn't manage clients. It doesn't generate financial reports. If you're a freelancer or small business owner, using Dext means you also need separate invoicing software, separate accounting software, and the time to keep all three in sync.

Invoicemonk takes a different approach: receipt scanning, expense tracking, invoicing, estimates, and accounting in a single platform. Your receipts feed directly into the same system as your invoices, giving you accurate profit-and-loss data without manual reconciliation. And it starts at free — compared to Dext's $20+/month starting price.

pricing.title: Pricing comparison

invoicemonk.title: Invoicemonk
invoicemonk.note: Receipt scanning + invoicing + expenses + accounting on every plan. No per-receipt limits on paid plans.

competitor.title: Dext
competitor.note: Receipt processing only. Requires separate invoicing and accounting software. Prices exclude accounting tool costs.

pricing.summary: Total cost with Dext: $20+/mo (Dext) + invoicing tool + accounting tool. Total cost with Invoicemonk: $0 (free tier covers all three).

invoicemonk.title: Choose Invoicemonk if you…
items: Want receipt scanning, invoicing, and accounting in one tool
Need a free starting point with comprehensive features
Prefer simplicity over maintaining multiple software subscriptions
Want expenses to automatically feed into your financial reports
Need multi-currency support for international business

competitor.title: Choose Dext if you…
items: Process very high volumes of receipts (hundreds per month)
Need the highest possible OCR accuracy for difficult receipts
Already use Xero or QuickBooks and want tight integration
Have an accountant who specifically requires Dext

migration.title: How to switch from Dext to Invoicemonk

steps.title: Export your Dext data
steps.desc: Download your receipt history and expense records from Dext. Export client/vendor lists if applicable.

steps.title: Sign up for Invoicemonk
steps.desc: Create a free account at app.invoicemonk.com. Set up your business profile, expense categories, and tax settings.

steps.title: Set up receipt workflows
steps.desc: Configure mobile scanning and email forwarding in Invoicemonk. Update your email filters to forward receipts to your new Invoicemonk receipt address.

steps.title: Import expense categories
steps.desc: Recreate your Dext categories in Invoicemonk. Set up auto-categorisation rules for your regular vendors.

steps.title: Cancel Dext (save $240+/year)
steps.desc: Once comfortable with Invoicemonk, cancel your Dext subscription. Your exported data serves as a historical reference.

items.question: Is Invoicemonk a good Dext alternative?
items.answer: For freelancers and small businesses — absolutely. Invoicemonk provides receipt scanning alongside invoicing, expenses, and accounting in one platform. You get more functionality at a lower cost. Dext remains the better choice for high-volume receipt processing or accountant firms managing many clients.

items.question: Is Dext's OCR better than Invoicemonk's?
items.answer: Dext has a slight edge in OCR accuracy for difficult receipts — faded thermal paper, handwritten receipts, and multi-language documents. For typical business receipts (printed, clear, in common languages), both tools perform well. The practical difference is minimal for most freelancers.

items.question: Can Invoicemonk replace both Dext and my invoicing software?
items.answer: Yes. Invoicemonk combines receipt scanning, expense tracking, invoicing, estimates, and accounting. If you're currently using Dext plus a separate invoicing tool, Invoicemonk replaces both — simplifying your workflow and reducing costs.

items.question: Does Dext include invoicing?
items.answer: No. Dext is exclusively a receipt capture and expense management tool. You need separate software for invoicing, estimates, and accounting. This means additional cost, additional setup, and manual data synchronisation between systems.

items.question: Which is better for freelancers: Dext or Invoicemonk?
items.answer: Invoicemonk is better for most freelancers because it combines everything in one tool at a lower price point. Freelancers typically don't need Dext's enterprise-grade receipt processing volume. The all-in-one approach saves time and money.

items.question: How much does Dext cost compared to Invoicemonk?
items.answer: Dext starts at $20/month for receipt processing only. Add invoicing software ($10–30/month) and accounting software ($10–30/month), and the total stack costs $40–80/month. Invoicemonk's free tier includes all three capabilities at $0/month.

cta.title: All-in-one receipt management + invoicing
cta.description: Stop paying for separate tools. Invoicemonk combines receipt scanning, expense tracking, invoicing, and accounting — starting free.
